pr.title: Update asmdiff paths, update some pointers in .data, add gobj header
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 50 made three infrastructure/decomp cleanup changes: it converted a function-pointer table in asm/data.s from raw address literals to named func_* symbols, added missing global labels at the corresponding fighter ASM function entry points, updated asmdiff.sh to diff against build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol, and added an initial src/sysdolphin/gobj.h header defining HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjProc. It also replaced an inline assembly fallback for HSD_FObjRemoveAll in src/sysdolphin/fobj.c with matching C using small inline helpers to shape codegen.
what_changed: Changed the lbl_803C1574 table in asm/data.s from raw .4byte address constants such as 0x800E14C8 and 0x80132F94 to symbolic references such as func_800E14C8 and func_80132F94.

Added .global labels and function labels at many fighter ASM entry points so the new .data symbolic pointers can resolve, including func_800E14C8, func_800E33E0, func_800E9F6C, func_800EC404, func_800F55D0, func_8010E234, func_8011108C, func_80116D04, func_8011C3C4, func_8011FB08, func_80124EAC, func_8012A2AC, func_8012F588, func_80132F94, func_801374A0, func_8013B6D8, func_8013D2A0, func_80142B88, func_8014677C, and func_8014C828.
Updated asmdiff.sh so the generated build output path is build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol instead of main.dol at the repository root.

Added src/sysdolphin/gobj.h with struct definitions for HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjProc, including callback function pointers, next/prev links, gx links, object/data pointers, and offset comments for several fields.
Replaced the NON_MATCHING/asm implementation split for HSD_FObjRemoveAll in src/sysdolphin/fobj.c with C code using inline helper functions HSD_FObjGetNext and HSD_FObjRemoveAll_Inlined.
Removed the large embedded assembly body for HSD_FObjRemoveAll and removed local #pragma push/peephole/pop directives around FObj state helpers.
smart_moves: Pointerizing asm/data.s made the table relocatable and searchable by symbol name instead of opaque numeric addresses, which helps later decomp work identify cross-file references.
The PR added matching global labels exactly where the pointer table needed them, avoiding unresolved symbols while preserving the existing ASM bodies.
The HSD_FObjRemoveAll rewrite used tiny inline helper functions to influence code generation rather than leaving the function as hand-written assembly.
The asmdiff.sh path update aligned the diff script with the build tree layout, reducing friction for local matching checks.
The new gobj.h concentrated HSD_GObj/HSD_GObjProc layout knowledge in a header, giving later sysdolphin work a reusable type definition rather than repeated anonymous pointers.
decomp_lessons: When a .data table contains function pointers, prefer symbolic .4byte func_* entries once corresponding ASM or C labels exist; this improves maintainability without changing the emitted pointer values.
Adding .global labels to existing ASM at exact function boundaries is a low-risk way to expose entry points for data references before those functions are decompiled.
For difficult matches, small inline accessors or wrapper helpers can shape compiler output enough to replace an asm block with C while keeping behavior straightforward.
Headers can be introduced early with partially understood fields when the layout is useful; offset comments and unknown placeholder names document confidence boundaries.
Keep tooling scripts in sync with generated build paths, especially as the project moves from flat outputs to versioned build directories such as build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol.
naming_conventions: Unknown function labels follow the address-derived func_XXXXXXXX convention, for example func_800E14C8 and func_8014C828.
Unknown or partially understood struct fields can use xNN_unk-style names such as x34_unk when the offset is known but semantic purpose is not.
Sysdolphin object types use HSD_* typedef names backed by underscored struct tags, for example typedef struct _HSD_GObj { ... } HSD_GObj and typedef struct _HSD_GObjProc { ... } HSD_GObjProc.
Header guard style in this PR used lowercase underscore guards, specifically _gobj_h_.
Function pointer callbacks in HSD_GObj were named by role, such as render_cb and user_data_remove_func.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted raw function-pointer literals in asm/data.s to named symbols after adding matching global labels in the relevant ASM files.
Inserted .global func_* labels immediately before existing instruction streams at known function entry addresses.
Removed an inline asm implementation of HSD_FObjRemoveAll once the C version was shaped to match.
Used inline HSD_FObjGetNext to force next-field loads through a helper and inline HSD_FObjRemoveAll_Inlined to express the recursive remove/free pattern in a codegen-friendly way.
Updated asmdiff.sh to objdump baserom.dol and build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol over the same start/stop address range.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that renamed the func_* labels added in PR 50 to semantic fighter callback names.
Check later revisions of src/sysdolphin/gobj.h for field name corrections or layout changes to HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjProc.
Look for later work that references lbl_803C1574 or identifies the purpose of the function-pointer table.
Search whether HSD_FObjRemoveAll remained matching after subsequent compiler or pragma changes.
Audit other .data pointer tables still using raw address literals that could be converted to symbolic labels.
